en.extract:
The buff-spotted flufftail (Sarothrura elegans) is a species of bird in the family Sarothruridae.
It is found in forested areas of Angola, Botswana, Burundi, Cameroon, Republic of the Congo, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Ivory Coast, Equatorial Guinea, Eswatini, Ethiopia, Gabon, Guinea, Kenya, Liberia, Malawi, Mozambique, Nigeria, Rwanda, Sierra Leone, Somalia, South Africa, South Sudan, Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia, and Zimbabwe.
Its diet is semi-omnivorous, consisting of various invertebrates as well as grass and tree seeds, which are thought to aid it in grinding other foods.
Its population is steady.

The '''buff-spotted flufftail''' ('''''Sarothrura elegans''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Sarothruridae]].
It is found in forested areas<ref>{{Cite journal |last1=Van Opstal |first1=Mattias |last2=Oosterlynck |first2=Bernard |last3=Belay |first3=Million |last4=Erens |first4=Jesse |last5=De Beenhouwer |first5=Matthias |date=2019-05-26 |title=First assessment of bird diversity in the UNESCO Sheka Forest Biosphere Reserve, southwestern Ethiopia: species richness, distribution and potential for avian conservation |journal=Journal of Threatened Taxa |volume=11 |issue=7 |pages=13850–13867 |doi=10.11609/jott.4726.11.7.13850-13867 |issn=0974-7907|doi-access=free }}</ref> of [[Angola]], [[Botswana]], [[Burundi]], [[Cameroon]], [[Republic of the Congo]], [[Democratic Republic of the Congo]], [[Ivory Coast]], [[Equatorial Guinea]], [[Eswatini]], [[Ethiopia]], [[Gabon]], [[Guinea]], [[Kenya]], [[Liberia]], [[Malawi]], [[Mozambique]], [[Nigeria]], [[Rwanda]], [[Sierra Leone]], [[Somalia]], [[South Africa]], [[South Sudan]], [[Tanzania]], [[Uganda]], [[Zambia]], and [[Zimbabwe]].

Its diet is semi-omnivorous, consisting of various invertebrates as well as grass and tree seeds, which are thought to aid it in grinding other foods.<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal |last1=Tarakini |first1=Tawanda |last2=Mabika |first2=Innocent |last3=Mwedzi |first3=Tongayi |last4=Mundy |first4=Peter |last5=Fritz |first5=Hervé |date=2021-05-11 |title=Diet and Life-History Traits of Savannah Dwelling Waterbirds in Southern Africa: Implications for Their Conservation Status |journal=Birds |volume=2 |issue=2 |pages=173–184 |doi=10.3390/birds2020013 |issn=2673-6004|doi-access=free |url=https://hal-cnrs.archives-ouvertes.fr/hal-03384413/file/Tarakinietal.2021Dietandlifehistrorytraits.pdf }}</ref><ref>del Hoyo, J., Elliott, A., and Sargatal, J. 1996. Handbook of the Birds of the World, vol. 3: Hoatzin to Auks. Lynx Edicions, Barcelona, Spain.</ref><ref>Hockey, P.A.R., Dean, W.R.J. and Ryan, P.G. 2005. Roberts birds of southern Africa. Trustees of the John Voelcker Bird Book Fund, Cape Town, South Africa.</ref>

Its population is steady.<ref name=":0" />
[[Image:Buff-spotted Flufftail.jpg|left|thumb|Specimen at Nairobi National Museum]]
